title: Syracuse lawmakers ‘disappointed’ with Walsh’s proposed tax hike, budget deficit
summary: Syracuse Common Council leadership expressed disappointment with Mayor Walsh's proposed FY2025 budget, which includes a $27 million deficit and 2% tax rate increase. Council finance chair Corey Williams disputes the deficit calculation, viewing the true shortfall as $34 million when accounting for tax revenue. The council will conduct line-by-line budget review over the coming month, with final adoption due May 8.

political_signal.description: Council finance chair and mayoral candidate express fiscal concerns and resistance to proposed tax increase; criticism centers on deficit spending sustainability and timing of tax hike amid resident economic anxiety.

processing_notes: Mechanical extraction included $1 million housing allocation and $7.5 million housing trust fund balance as key contested line items
Pat Hogan identified as mayoral candidate—potential mayoral race signal
Fund balance context (current $120M vs. $40M at Walsh administration start) suggests strong reserve position but council skeptical of continued draw-down strategy
Public hearing scheduled April 29; final budget adoption May 8—key decision points
Last year's budget received 30 amendments—precedent for substantial council modifications
